access/bot-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining bot access in app-interface.
This includes details about the bot's usernames, service accounts,
and associated roles.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the bot.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the bot.

properties.name: org_username
properties.description: The GitLab username associated with the bot.

properties.name: github_username
properties.description: The GitHub username associated with the bot.

properties.name: openshift_serviceaccount
properties.description:
The OpenShift service account associated with the bot,
in the format `<namespace>/<serviceaccount>`.

properties.name: quay_username
properties.description: The Quay username associated with the bot.

properties.name: owner
properties.description: Reference to the owner of the bot.

properties.name: roles
properties.description: A list of roles assigned to the bot.

acs/acs-policy-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining security policies in Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security (ACS).
Policies are used to enforce security standards and best practices in Kubernetes
clusters. This schema includes details about policy severity, categories, scope,
integrations, and conditions.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the policy, explaining its purpose and functionality.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the policy. This should be unique and descriptive.

properties.name: integrations
properties.description:
Configuration for integrations associated with the policy, such as
notifiers for external systems.

properties.name: severity
properties.description:
The severity level of the policy. This indicates the importance of the
policy and the potential impact of violations.

properties.name: categories
properties.description:
A list of categories that the policy belongs to. Categories help organize
policies based on their purpose or focus area.

properties.name: scope
properties.description:
The scope of the policy, defining the level (cluster or namespace) and
the specific clusters or namespaces it applies to.

properties.name: conditions
properties.description:
A list of conditions that define the criteria for policy violations.
Each condition specifies a rule that must be met for the policy to trigger.

acs/acs-policy-condition-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining conditions in Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security (ACS) policies.
Conditions specify the criteria that must be met for a policy to trigger.
This schema includes details about fields, comparisons, and thresholds
used to evaluate policy violations.

properties.name: policyField
properties.description:
The field in the policy that is being evaluated. Examples include
`cvss`, `severity`, `cve`, `image_tag`, and `image_age`.

properties.name: comparison
properties.description:
The comparison operator used to evaluate the policy condition.
Supported operators include `gt` (greater than), `gte` (greater than or equal to),
`eq` (equal to), `lt` (less than), and `lte` (less than or equal to).

properties.name: score
properties.description: The numerical score used for comparison when the `policyField` is `cvss`.

properties.name: level
properties.description:
The severity level used for comparison when the `policyField` is `severity`.
Supported levels include `low`, `moderate`, `important`, and `critical`.

properties.name: fixable
properties.description:
Indicates whether the condition applies only to fixable issues.
This is used when the `policyField` is `cve`.

properties.name: tags
properties.description: A list of image tags used for evaluation when the `policyField` is `image_tag`.

properties.name: negate
properties.description:
Indicates whether the condition should negate the specified tags.
This is used when the `policyField` is `image_tag`.

properties.name: days
properties.description: The number of days used for evaluation when the `policyField` is `image_age`.

app-interface/app-interface-email-1.yml.description:
Specification for a single automated email message.
Recipients can be static or dynamic based on service, cluster, namespace, aws account, role, or user relations.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Internal name for an email instantiation.

properties.name: subject
properties.description: Subject line of email message

properties.name: to
properties.description: Recipients of email message

properties.name: body
properties.description: Body of email message

app-interface/app-interface-settings-1.y.description:
Specifications for configuring an app-interface instance.
Configuration includes repository configuration, credentials, integrations, and other operational parameters.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the app-interface settings object.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the app-interface settings object.

properties.name: repoUrl
properties.description: URL of the app-interface Git repository.

properties.name: vault
properties.description:
If true, a Vault instance provides secrets for automation.
If false, a config file provides secrets for automation.

properties.name: kubeBinary
properties.description: The Kubernetes CLI binary to use (e.g., kubectl or oc).

properties.name: mergeRequestGateway
properties.description: Determines the interface and destination for merge requests created with automation.

properties.name: saasDeployJobTemplate
properties.description: Used with visual-qontract to generate pipeline names.

properties.name: hashLength
properties.description: The length of the hash used in openshift-saas-deploy pipeline

properties.name: smtp
properties.description: SMTP configuration for sending emails.

properties.name: imap
properties.description: IMAP configuration for receiving emails.

properties.name: githubRepoInvites
properties.description: Configuration for the github-repo-invites integration.

properties.name: ldap
properties.description: LDAP configuration for authentication and directory services.

properties.name: dependencies
properties.description: List of dependencies required by the app-interface instance.

properties.name: credentials
properties.description: List of GraphQL client credential instantiations.

properties.name: sqlQuery
properties.description: Configuration for SQL queries.

properties.name: pushGatewayCluster
properties.description: Reference to the cluster used for the push gateway.

properties.name: alertingServices
properties.description: List of services authorized to send alerts.

properties.name: endpointMonitoringBlackboxExporterModules
properties.description: List of supported blackbox exporter modules for endpoint monitoring.

properties.name: jiraWatcher
properties.description: Configuration for the Jira watcher integration.

properties.name: state
properties.description: Configuration for app-interface state settings.

properties.name: ldapGroups
properties.description: Configuration for LDAP groups.

properties.name: deadMansSnitchSettings
properties.description: Configuration for Dead Man's Snitch integration.

properties.name: costReport
properties.description: Configuration for cost reporting.

properties.name: jiralert
properties.description: Configuration for Jira alerting.

properties.name: rhcsProvider
properties.description: Configuration for RHCSv2 certificate generation and storing

properties.name: customMessages
properties.description: Custom messages used in integrations.

properties.name: terraformResourcesProviderExclusions
properties.description: Exclusions for Terraform resources by provider.

properties.name: terraformResourcesProviderExclusionsByProvisioner
properties.description: Deprecated object for Terraform resource exclusions by provisioner.

properties.name: imagePatternsBlockRules
properties.description:
Configuration to block specific image patterns from being deployed to environments
matching the label selector. This allows blocking non-compliant images (e.g.,
redhat-user-workloads) from production environments.

app-interface/app-interface-slack-notifi.description:
Schema for defining a single Slack notification message.
Notifications can be sent to specific channels or users.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the Slack notification object.

properties.name: subject
properties.description: The subject or title of the Slack notification message.

properties.name: channel
properties.description: The Slack channel where the notification will be sent.

properties.name: to
properties.description: Recipients of the Slack notification.

properties.name: body
properties.description: The body content of the Slack notification message.

app-interface/app-interface-sql-query-1..description:
Schema for defining SQL queries for automated execution.
Includes details about the query, its schedule, output, and overrides.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the SQL query object, following Kubernetes container naming conventions.

properties.name: namespace
properties.description: Reference to the namespace where the SQL query will be executed.

properties.name: identifier
properties.description: The identifier for the RDS instance as defined in a namespace instantiation.

properties.name: requestor
properties.description: Reference to the user who requested the SQL query.

properties.name: overrides
properties.description: Overrides for database connection parameters.

properties.name: output
properties.description:
The output method for the SQL query results. Options include:
- `stdout`: Print results to standard output.
- `filesystem`: Save results to the filesystem.
- `encrypted`: Save results in an encrypted format.

properties.name: schedule
properties.description: The schedule for executing the SQL query, defined in cron format.

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Indicates whether the SQL query should be deleted after execution.

properties.name: query
properties.description: The SQL query to be executed.

properties.name: queries
properties.description: A list of SQL queries to be executed.

app-interface/change-type-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining change types in app-interface.
Change types influence how merge requests are processed and provide
context for changes, including priority, restrictions, and inheritance.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the change type.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the change type.

properties.name: priority
properties.description:
The priority of the change type, which influences the order
in which merge requests are processed.

properties.name: contextType
properties.description: The type of context associated with the change type.

properties.name: contextSchema
properties.description: The schema defining the context for the change type.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description:
Indicates whether the change type is disabled.
A disabled change type does not have any effect but will still
log into the pull request check logs.

properties.name: restrictive
properties.description:
Indicates whether the change type is restrictive.
Entities protected with a restrictive change type can only be
changed by the team assigned to the change type.

properties.name: inherit
properties.description:
A list of additional change types whose changes are inherited
by this change type.

properties.name: changes
properties.description:
A list of changes associated with the change type. Each change
specifies a provider, schema, and additional context.

properties.name: implicitOwnership
properties.description:
A list of implicit ownership rules for the change type.
These rules define ownership based on JSONPath selectors.

app-interface/credentials-request-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a credentials request in app-interface.
This includes details about the user requesting the credentials
and the credentials being requested.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the credentials request object.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A brief description of the credentials request.

properties.name: user
properties.description: Reference to the user who is requesting the credentials.

properties.name: credentials
properties.description: The credentials being requested.

app-interface/database-access-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining database access in app-interface.
This includes details about the user, database, and access permissions.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the database access object.

properties.name: username
properties.description: The username for the database access.

properties.name: database
properties.description: The name of the database to which access is being granted.

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Indicates whether the database access should be deleted.

properties.name: access
properties.description: A list of access permissions for the database.

app-interface/deletion-approval-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining deletion approvals in app-interface.
This includes details about the resource being deleted,
the type of resource, and the expiration of the approval.

properties.name: expiration
properties.description:
The expiration date for the deletion approval.
After this date, the approval is no longer valid.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of resource for which deletion is being approved.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the resource for which deletion is being approved.

app-interface/graphql-schemas-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining GraphQL configurations in app-interface.
This includes details about GraphQL fields, interfaces, and
strategies for resolving data.

properties.name: confs
properties.description: A list of GraphQL configurations.

app-interface/prometheus-rule-test-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining Prometheus rule tests in app-interface.
This includes configurations for rule files, evaluation intervals,
test cases, and expected outputs.

properties.name: rule_files
properties.description: A list of Prometheus rule files to be tested.

properties.name: evaluation_interval
properties.description: The interval at which Prometheus evaluates the rules.

properties.name: group_eval_order
properties.description: The order in which rule groups are evaluated.

properties.name: tests
properties.description: A list of test cases for Prometheus rules.

app-interface/query-validation-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining query validation configurations in app-interface.
This includes details about the queries to validate, escalation policies,
and associated resources.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A unique name for the query validation configuration.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of the queries included in the file.

properties.name: escalationPolicy
properties.description: Reference to the escalation policy to be used if validation fails.

properties.name: queries
properties.description: A list of queries to validate.

properties.name: resources
properties.description: A list of resources associated with the query validation.

app-interface/resource-template-test-1.y.description:
Schema for defining resource template tests in app-interface.
This includes details about the resource template to be tested,
the expected result, and other metadata.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A unique name for the resource template test.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of the resource template test.

properties.name: resourcePath
properties.description: The path to the resource template to be tested.

properties.name: expectedResult
properties.description: The expected result of the templating process to compare against.

app-interface/template-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining templates in app-interface.
This includes details about the template, its target path,
conditions, tests, and rendering options.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A unique name for the template.

properties.name: autoApproved
properties.description: Indicates whether the template is automatically approved.

properties.name: targetPath
properties.description: The target path where the template will be applied.

properties.name: condition
properties.description: A condition that must be met for the template to be applied.

properties.name: overwrite
properties.description: whether to overwrite if file in target path or identifier in patch already exist.

properties.name: patch
properties.description: A patch to be applied to the template.

properties.name: template
properties.description: The content of the template to be rendered.

properties.name: templateTest
properties.description: A list of tests to validate the template.

properties.name: templateRenderOptions
properties.description: Options for rendering the template.

app-interface/template-collection-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a collection of templates in app-interface.
This includes details about the templates, variables, and
configurations for generating multiple templates.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A unique name for the template collection.

properties.name: additionalMrLabels
properties.description: Additional merge request labels to be applied to the templates.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of the template collection.

properties.name: enableAutoApproval
properties.description: Indicates whether auto-approval is enabled for the template collection.

properties.name: forEach
properties.description: Configuration for iterating over a list of items to generate templates.

properties.name: variables
properties.description: Variables to be used in the templates.

properties.name: templates
properties.description: A list of templates to be included in the collection.

app-interface/template-test-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining tests for templates in app-interface.
This includes details about the variables, expected outputs,
and conditions for validating template rendering.

properties.name: name
properties.description: A unique name for the template test.

properties.name: variables
properties.description: Variables to be used in the template test.

properties.name: current
properties.description: The current content of the file to be templated.

properties.name: expectedTargetPath
properties.description: The expected result of the target path after rendering the template.

properties.name: expectedOutput
properties.description: The expected output of the template after rendering.

properties.name: expectedToRender
properties.description: Expected evaluation result of `$.condition` in `/app-interface/template-1.yml`.

properties.name: grafanaUrls
properties.description:
List of links to Grafana Dashboard/Folders associated with the service. Please provide
a title together with the link.

properties.name: sopsUrl
properties.description:
Link to the git repo path that contains the top-level for the service
SOPs.

properties.name: architectureDocument
properties.description:
The Architecture Document should be written with SREs as the target audience. Anyone
reading the Architecture Document should understand how the service works, even
without any prior background. This document will be the primary reference during
incidents. To ensure the Architecture Document is always accessible to SREs and its
changes properly reviewed and tracked, it must be written in markdown and stored in
a place where SREs can control its changes.

properties.name: serviceOwners
properties.description: Teams or individuals who is/are responsible for the running instance of the software.

properties.name: serviceNotifications
properties.description: Teams or individuals who is/are should get notifications related to the service.

properties.name: serviceDocs
properties.description: List of service docs

properties.name: appCode
properties.description: The CMDB Application ID of the application or workload.

properties.name: costCenter
properties.description: Deprecated! Use /app-sre/environment-1.yml costCenter instead!

app-sre/app-changelog-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining changelogs for applications defined in app-interface.
This includes details about the application, the dates of changes,
and descriptions of the changes made.

properties.name: app
properties.description: Reference to the application associated with the changelog.

properties.name: changelog
properties.description:
A list of changelog entries for the application. Each entry includes
the date of the change and a list of changes made.

app-sre/automated-action-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining automated actions. Automated actions
represent predefined operations that can be executed programmatically,
with configurable retries, operation limits, and descriptions.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The automated action type, which indicates the kind of operation
being performed.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A brief description of the automated action, explaining its purpose
and functionality.

properties.name: instances
properties.description: A list of instances where the automated action can be executed.

properties.name: maxOps
properties.description:
The maximum number of operations that can be performed per hour. This
is used to limit the frequency of the automated action to prevent
overloading systems or exceeding quotas.

properties.name: arguments
properties.description: Action arguments that can be used to customize the behavior of the action.

app-sre/automated-actions-instance-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining instances of automated actions.
Each instance represents a deployment of automated actions with
associated metadata, such as name, description, and deployment details.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the automated actions instance. This name is used
to identify the instance across the system.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A brief description of the automated actions instance, explaining its
purpose and functionality.

properties.name: deployment
properties.description:
Reference to the OpenShift namespace where the automated actions
instance is deployed. This will be replaced by proper URL and token
references in the future.

app-sre/contract-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining contracts.
Contracts are agreements between the platform operator and platform tenants.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the contract. This should be a unique identifier that
clearly represents the purpose or scope of the contract.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the contract, explaining its purpose,
scope, and any relevant details.

properties.name: source
properties.description:
The location of the contract. This should be a valid URI pointing
to the source of the contract, such as a document or repository.

app-sre/contract-version-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a contract-1.yml schema version.
Each version represents a specific iteration of a contract, including its name,
description, associated contract, and source location.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the contract version. This should uniquely identify the
version and indicate its purpose or scope.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the contract version, explaining its purpose,
scope, and any relevant details.

properties.name: contract
properties.description:
Reference to the contract that this version is associated with. This
links the version to its parent contract.

properties.name: source
properties.description:
The location of the contract version. This should be a valid URI pointing
to the source of the version, such as a document or repository.

app-sre/environment-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining environments in an app-interface instance.
Environments represent logical groupings of resources and configurations for applications,
including their name, description, product association, parameters, and dependencies.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The unique name of the environment.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the environment, explaining its purpose
and scope.

properties.name: product
properties.description: Reference to the product associated with the environment.

properties.name: parameters
properties.description:
A set of key-value pairs representing environment-level parameters.
These parameters are used to configure the environment.

properties.name: secretParameters
properties.description: A list of environment-level parameters sourced from vault secrets.

properties.name: dependsOn
properties.description:
Reference to another environment that this environment depends on.
This is used to define dependencies between environments.

properties.name: servicePhase
properties.description: The phase of the service lifecycle that this environment represents.

properties.name: costCenter
properties.description: The 3-digit code identifying the Cost Center funding the application resources.

app-sre/escalation-policy-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining escalation policies for services. Escalation policies
specify how incidents or issues are escalated, including the channels
and configurations for notifications, such as Slack, email, PagerDuty,
and Jira.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The unique name of the escalation policy.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A description of the escalation policy, explaining its purpose
and scope.

properties.name: channels
properties.description:
The notification channels configured for the escalation policy. These
channels specify how and where notifications are sent during an escalation.

app-sre/gabi-instance-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a GABI (go-auditable-db-interface) instance.
A GABI instance allows tenants to send SQL commands to their production
databases for troubleshooting, with auditing via Splunk. This schema
supports requesting a waiver for the read replica requirement,
and tracks signoff managers, users, and deployment instances.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the GABI instance. Used to identify the instance
across the system.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the GABI instance, including its purpose
and any relevant context.

properties.name: readReplicasWaiverPledge
properties.description:
Text pledge provided by the tenant when requesting a waiver for the
read replica requirement. This should outline the risks accepted by
the tenant if the waiver is granted.

properties.name: signoffManagers
properties.description:
A list of users who have managerial authority to approve the waiver
for the read replica requirement. Each entry references a user.

properties.name: users
properties.description:
A list of users authorized to access this GABI instance. Each entry
references a user.

properties.name: instances
properties.description:
A list of deployment instances for this GABI configuration. Each
instance specifies the AWS account, identifier, and namespace.

properties.name: expirationDate
properties.description: The expiration date for GABI instance approval in YYYY-MM-DD format.

properties.name: cache
properties.description: integration requires cache

properties.name: command
properties.description: command to run

properties.name: disableUnleash
properties.description: disable integration interaction with unleash instance

properties.name: environmentAware
properties.description: integration is aware of the environment it is running in

properties.name: extraArgs
properties.description: additional arguments to pass to integration

properties.name: internalCertificates
properties.description: integration requires internal certificates to execute

properties.name: logs
properties.description: ship logs to providers (cloudwatch is enabled by default)

properties.name: sleepDurationSecs
properties.description: time to sleep in seconds between integration executions

properties.name: state
properties.description: integration is stateful

properties.name: storage
properties.description: size of cache storage

properties.name: storageClassName
properties.description: kubernetes storage class name for the cache

properties.name: trigger
properties.description: integration is an openshift-saas-deploy trigger

properties.name: cron
properties.description: cron expression for integration execution

properties.name: dashdotdb
properties.description: integration interacts with dashdotdb

properties.name: concurrencyPolicy
properties.description: how to treat concurrent executions of the integration

properties.name: restartPolicy
properties.description: restarts of the integration

properties.name: successfulJobHistoryLimit
properties.description: number of history records reserved for successful integration executions

properties.name: failedJobHistoryLimit
properties.description: number of history records reserved for failed integration executions

properties.name: enablePushgateway
properties.description: push metrics at the end of the run to the configured Pushgateway

app-sre/maintenance-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining service maintenances. Maintenances represent planned
or ongoing service interruptions or changes, including their name, message,
affected services, scheduled time window, and announcement configuration.
This schema enables tenants to self-service maintenance announcements on
status pages and to communicate maintenance details to AppSRE and stakeholders.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the maintenance. Used to identify and reference
the maintenance event across the system.

properties.name: message
properties.description:
A message describing the maintenance, its purpose, or its expected
impact on services.

properties.name: affectedServices
properties.description:
A list of services affected by this maintenance. Each entry references
an application that will be impacted during the maintenance window.

properties.name: scheduledStart
properties.description:
The scheduled start time for the maintenance window, in ISO 8601
date-time format.

properties.name: scheduledEnd
properties.description:
The scheduled end time for the maintenance window, in ISO 8601
date-time format.

properties.name: announcements
properties.description:
A list of announcement configurations for this maintenance. Each
announcement specifies a provider (such as statuspage), the page to
announce on, and notification options for subscribers.

app-sre/network-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a network reservation. Networks represent CIDR blocks
that can be reserved for use by VPCs or other resources. This schema enables
tracking of network allocations, parent-child relationships between networks,
and the entity currently using the network. It supports automation and
visibility for network management in cloud environments.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the network. Used to identify the network reservation
across the system.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the network, its intended use, or any
relevant context for the reservation.

properties.name: inUseBy
properties.description:
Reference to the entity that has claimed this network. If the network
is unclaimed, this value will be null. Used to track which resource
or team is currently using the network.

properties.name: networkAddress
properties.description:
The network address in CIDR format (e.g., 10.0.200.0/24). This defines
the range of IP addresses covered by this network reservation.

properties.name: parentNetwork
properties.description:
Reference to the parent network, if this network is a subdivision of
a larger block. If this is a root network, this property will be null.

properties.name: region
properties.description: The cloud provider region where this network resides.

app-sre/pgp-reencrypt-settings-1.yml.description:
Schema for configuring PGP re-encryption settings. This schema defines
the keys and vault paths used for re-encrypting sensitive data, such as
AWS account passwords, with a central APP-SRE PGP key. It also allows
specifying AWS accounts to skip re-encryption and use an alternative
notification method. These settings help manage secure key rotation and
ensure users are notified of expiring or invalid keys.

properties.name: public_gpg_key
properties.description:
The PGP public key owned by APP-SRE, used to encrypt user data
instead of individual user keys. This enables centralized encryption
management and simplifies key rotation.

properties.name: private_pgp_key_vault_path
properties.description:
The vault path to the private PGP key used for decrypting and
re-encrypting data. This key must be kept secure and accessible
only to authorized integrations.

properties.name: reencrypt_vault_path
properties.description:
The base vault path where PGP-encrypted entries are read from and
written to during the re-encryption process.

properties.name: aws_account_output_vault_path
properties.description:
The vault path where re-encrypted AWS account passwords are written.
This ensures that updated credentials are securely stored.

properties.name: skip_aws_accounts
properties.description:
A list of AWS accounts to skip during the re-encryption process.
Access requests for these accounts will use the SMTP mail send
implementation in the terraform-users integration instead of PGP
re-encryption.

app-sre/pipelines-provider-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a pipelines provider. Pipelines providers declare
configuration for an automated deployment pipeline service used for specific tenant
services and can be used to define different types of providers depending on the
specific technology used.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the pipelines provider. Used to identify the provider
across the system.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the pipelines provider, including its purpose
and any relevant context.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: The type of pipelines provider. Currently, only 'tekton' is supported.

properties.name: defaults
properties.description:
Reference to the default configuration for this pipelines provider.
This declares the default settings for the pipeline execution,
which can be overridden in the current pipelines provider.

properties.name: namespace
properties.description:
Reference to the OpenShift namespace where the pipelines provider
resources will be deployed.

properties.name: taskTemplates
properties.description:
A list of Tekton Task jinja2 templates that will be used to create
the corresponding objects in the provider namespace.

properties.name: pipelineTemplates
properties.description:
Tekton Pipeline templates that will be used to create the
corresponding objects in the provider namespace. This object defines
the pipeline templates that are supported by the provider.

properties.name: deployResources
properties.description:
CPU and memory resources used by the openshift-saas-deploy step of the
openshift-saas-deploy tasks created by the openshift-tekton-resources
integration.

properties.name: productOwners
properties.description: Teams or individuals who is/are responsible for the running instance of the software.

app-sre/report-1.yml.description:
[DEPRECATED] Schema for defining a report. This schema is not actively
used. There are no current plans for use, but it may be referenced
for legacy data or future platform enhancements.

properties.name: app
properties.description:
Reference to the application this report is associated with. This
links the report to its parent app for context and traceability.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the report. Used to identify the report within
the context of the application.

properties.name: date
properties.description:
The date the report was generated or is relevant for, in ISO 8601
format (YYYY-MM-DD).

properties.name: contentFormatVersion
properties.description:
The version of the report content format, following semantic versioning
(e.g., 1.0.0). This helps consumers interpret the structure of the content.

properties.name: content
properties.description:
The main content of the report. This may be plain text, markdown, or
another format as defined by the content format version.

properties.name: secretParameters
properties.description: saas file level parameters from vault secrets

properties.name: validateTargetsInApp
properties.description: indicates a desire to validate that all targets belong to the same app as the saas file itself

properties.name: validatePlannedData
properties.description: enable skipping planned data validation, mostly for automated bootstrap pusrposes

properties.name: deprecated
properties.description: prevent updates to a saas file

properties.name: timeout
properties.description:
Maximum time the deployment job is allowed to last. In Tekton it defaults to
60 minutes. We don't allow smaller times as Tekton implementation currently deletes
PipelineRun pods, hence destroying logs.

properties.name: deployResources
properties.description: CPU and memory resources used by the openshift-saas-deploy step of the openshift-saas-deploy tasks created by the openshit-tekton-resources integration

app-sre/saas-file-authentication-1.yml.description: saas file authentication section for image and/or code credentials

properties.name: secretParameters
properties.description: target level parameters from vault secrets

properties.name: images
properties.description: wait for all images to exist before triggering a deployment

properties.name: slos
properties.description: list of file path of slo documents to be evaluated before promoting changes

app-sre/scorecard-2.yml.description:
Schema for defining a self-service scorecard for limited-scope service engagements.
The scorecard helps service owners assess their maturity and reliability
based on well-defined acceptance criteria established by AppSRE for
production services. Each scorecard is a self-assessment, to be filled
in and maintained by service owners, and is intended to be visible to
all stakeholders. The scorecard enables AppSRE to raise awareness and
provide recommendations for improving service maturity.

properties.name: app
properties.description:
Reference to the application this scorecard is associated with.
Links the scorecard to its parent app for context and traceability.

properties.name: date
properties.description: The date the scorecard was completed or updated, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

properties.name: acceptanceCriteria
properties.description:
A list of acceptance criteria items representing the maturity and
reliability checks for the service. Each item corresponds to a
well-defined criterion, its current status, and an optional comment.
All 37 criteria must be included for a complete assessment.

app-sre/slo-document-1.yml.description:
Schema for documenting Service Level Objectives (SLOs) for an application or
component. This schema enables teams to transparently define, track, and
communicate SLOs, including SLI definitions, targets, time windows, and
justifications. SLO documents help drive reliability, set user expectations,
and support automated alerting and reporting.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the SLO document. Used to identify this document
across the system.

properties.name: app
properties.description: Reference to the application this SLO document applies to.

properties.name: namespaces
properties.description:
A list of namespaces where this SLO document applies. Each entry
specifies the target namespace, optional SLO namespace for alerting,
and Prometheus access configuration.

properties.name: slos
properties.description:
A list of SLO definitions for this service or component. Each SLO
specifies the SLI type, specification, target, time window, and
supporting details such as Prometheus expressions and dashboards.

app-sre/sre-checkpoint-1.yml.description:
Schema for documenting an SRE checkpoint for a service. SRE checkpoints are
periodic reviews performed by AppSRE to assess service reliability, adherence
to best practices, and operational readiness. The process includes verifying
metadata, SLOs, SOPs, and reliability, and tracking improvements via JIRA.
This schema enables traceability of checkpoint events, responsible engineers,
and contract versions.

properties.name: app
properties.description:
Reference to the application that is the subject of this SRE checkpoint.
Links the checkpoint to its parent app for context and traceability.

properties.name: sre
properties.description: Reference to the AppSRE engineer responsible for conducting this checkpoint.

properties.name: date
properties.description: The date the SRE checkpoint was performed, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

properties.name: issue
properties.description: The JIRA issue key tracking this SRE checkpoint (e.g., APPSRE-12345).

properties.name: contractVersion
properties.description:
Reference to the contract version in effect at the time of the checkpoint.
This links the checkpoint to the set of requirements and expectations
evaluated during the review.

app-sre/tekton-pipeline-template-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Tekton pipeline template. Tekton pipeline templates
are reusable definitions for CI/CD pipelines that can be deployed in
Tekton pipeline provider namespaces. This schema allows specifying the
template's name, type, file path, and variables for parameterization.
It supports both per-SaaS file and per-namespace pipeline templates,
with naming constraints to ensure compatibility with Tekton object limits.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Tekton pipeline template. Naming constraints
apply based on the template type to ensure compatibility with Tekton
object name length limits.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of pipeline template. Use 'onePerSaasFile' for templates
that are unique per SaaS file, or 'onePerNamespace' for templates
that are unique per namespace.

properties.name: path
properties.description: The file path to the pipeline template definition.

properties.name: variables
properties.description:
A mapping of variable names to values for parameterizing the pipeline
template. Variables can be used to customize pipeline behavior for
different deployments.

app-sre/tekton-provider-defaults-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining default configuration values for Tekton pipeline providers.
These defaults ensure that all required fields for a Tekton provider are present,
reducing duplication and simplifying provider definitions. Providers can override
any field as needed, but will always inherit a complete set of defaults for
task and pipeline templates, and deployment resources.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Tekton provider defaults configuration. Used to
identify and reference this set of defaults.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the defaults configuration, including its
intended use and any relevant context.

properties.name: taskTemplates
properties.description:
A list of Tekton Task jinja2 templates that will be used to create
the corresponding objects in the provider namespace.

properties.name: pipelineTemplates
properties.description:
Tekton Pipeline jinja2 templates that will be used to create the
corresponding objects in the provider namespace. This object defines
the types of pipeline templates that are supported. Properties of this
object will be the different pipelines supported in qontract-reconcile.

properties.name: deployResources
properties.description:
CPU and memory resources used by the openshift-saas-deploy step of the
openshift-saas-deploy tasks created by the openshift-tekton-resources
integration.

app-sre/tekton-task-template-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Tekton task template. Tekton task templates are reusable
definitions for CI/CD tasks that can be deployed in Tekton pipeline provider
namespaces. This schema allows specifying the template's name, type, file path,
and variables for parameterization. It supports both per-SaaS file and per-namespace
task templates, with naming constraints to ensure compatibility with Tekton object
limits.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Tekton task template. Naming constraints apply based
on the template type to ensure compatibility with Tekton object name length
limits.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of task template. Use 'onePerSaasFile' for templates that are unique
per SaaS file, or 'onePerNamespace' for templates that are unique per namespace.

properties.name: path
properties.description:
The file path to the task template definition. This should be a relative path
within the repository where the template is stored.

properties.name: variables
properties.description:
A mapping of variable names to values for parameterizing the task template.
Variables can be used to customize task behavior for different deployments.

properties.name: allowUnmanagedFeatureToggles
properties.description: Set to true to allow unmanaged/manually created feature toggles

app-sre/vault-secret-parameter-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a parameter sourced from a Vault secret. These parameters
are used to inject sensitive values, such as credentials or tokens, into
OpenShift templates or Helm charts during deployment. Parameter names must
start with a letter and may only contain letters, numbers, and underscores,
to comply with OpenShift template requirements. This enables secure and
automated handling of secrets in deployment pipelines.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the parameter to be injected. Must start with a letter and
contain only letters, numbers, and underscores. This restriction ensures
compatibility with OpenShift template parameter naming requirements.

properties.name: secret
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the value for this parameter.
The secret will be retrieved and injected at deployment time.

properties.name: automationRole
properties.description: Role per integration or use-case to assume in the account

properties.name: partition
properties.description: the partition used in ARNs in the account (arn:aws:...)

properties.name: terraformState
properties.description: key information for a terraform's state location and integration

properties.name: rosa
properties.description: Rosa related attributes in the aws account.

properties.name: billingAccount
properties.description: AWS account to be used as the Billing account for ROSA HCP cluster provisioning

properties.name: sso
properties.description: Enable single sign on for the account. Default is enabled.

properties.name: alias
properties.description: The alias for the account. This is the name that appears in the AWS SSO page. If not set, the account name will be used.

properties.name: organizationAccountTags
properties.description: For payer accounts only! Apply these tags to all accounts in the organization. This is useful for tagging all accounts in the organization with the same tags.

properties.name: externalResources
properties.description: External resources settings for the account.

aws/account-request-1.yml.description:
Schema for requesting the creation of a new AWS account. This schema captures
all required information for AppSRE to automate AWS account creation, including
account name, owner, organization, quota limits, and additional features.
Account requests are processed by integrations to provision accounts, assign
organizational units, set tags, and configure initial quotas. This enables
GitOps-driven, auditable, and self-service AWS account management.
After successful account creation, automation deletes the
`account-request-1.yml` file and adds `account-1.yml` file.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
Unique name for the AWS account. This will be used as the account alias
and must be between 3 and 63 characters, containing only digits, lowercase
letters, and hyphens (-). The name cannot begin or end with a hyphen.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A detailed description of the AWS account, its intended use, or any
relevant context for the request.

properties.name: accountOwner
properties.description:
Information about the owner of the AWS account. The owner is responsible
for the account and will be the main point of contact.

properties.name: organization
properties.description:
Reference to the AWS organization where the account will be created.
Includes payer account, organizational unit, and tags.

properties.name: quotaLimits
properties.description:
A list of initial AWS quota limits to set for this account. Each entry
references a quota limits object, specifying service and quota codes.

properties.name: resourcesDefaultRegion
properties.description: The default AWS region for resources created in this account.

properties.name: supportedDeploymentRegions
properties.description: A list of AWS regions where deployments are supported for this account.

properties.name: uid
properties.description:
The AWS account UID. If specified, this request will take over an existing
account instead of creating a new one. Ensure the current owner and
organization are specified.

properties.name: additionalFeatures
properties.description:
Enable or disable additional features for this account. Refer to the
account template for all available feature options.

properties.name: accountFileTargetPath
properties.description:
The target path for the new account file in app-interface. Defaults to
`/aws/<account_name>/account.yml` if not specified.

aws/asg-defaults-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining default configuration values for AWS Auto Scaling Groups (ASGs).
This schema enables teams to specify baseline settings for scaling, instance types,
networking, security, and lifecycle management. These defaults are used to ensure
consistent and reliable ASG deployments across environments.

properties.name: max_size
properties.description: The maximum number of instances allowed in the Auto Scaling Group.

properties.name: min_size
properties.description: The minimum number of instances to maintain in the Auto Scaling Group.

properties.name: iam_role_name
properties.description: The name of the IAM role to associate with instances in the ASG.

properties.name: instance_types
properties.description: A list of EC2 instance types that can be launched by the ASG.

properties.name: vpc_zone_identifier
properties.description: A list of subnet IDs for the ASG. Instances will be launched in these subnets.

properties.name: capacity_rebalance
properties.description:
If true, enables capacity rebalancing to proactively replace instances
at risk of interruption (for Spot Instances).

properties.name: max_instance_lifetime
properties.description:
The maximum lifetime (in seconds) for instances in the ASG. Instances
exceeding this age will be terminated and replaced.

properties.name: protect_from_scale_in
properties.description: If true, protects instances from being terminated during scale-in events.

properties.name: enabled_metrics
properties.description: A list of metrics to enable for the ASG. Metrics are reported to CloudWatch.

properties.name: instances_distribution
properties.description:
Configuration for distributing instances between On-Demand and Spot capacity.
Controls allocation strategies and pool sizes for cost optimization.

properties.name: instance_refresh_preferences
properties.description: Preferences for instance refresh operations, such as rolling updates.

properties.name: vpc_security_group_ids
properties.description: A list of security group IDs to associate with the ASG instances.

properties.name: update_default_version
properties.description: If true, updates the default launch template version for the ASG.

properties.name: block_device_mappings
properties.description:
A list of block device mappings for the ASG instances, specifying
device names and EBS volume configurations.

properties.name: instance_requirements
properties.description:
Requirements for EC2 instance selection, such as vCPU and memory limits,
and excluded instance types.

properties.name: metadata_options
properties.description: Customize the metadata options for the instance.

aws/cleanup-option-1.yml.description: Define cleanup options of AWS objects.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Type of cleanup to implement.

properties.name: regex
properties.description: Regex expression to filter items by.

properties.name: age
properties.description: AMI age from which we can consider deletion.

properties.name: retention_in_days
properties.description: Cloudwatch log retention

properties.name: region
properties.description: AWS region.

properties.name: delete_empty_log_group
properties.description: Enable deletion when log group is empty.

properties.name: apply_immediately
properties.description: Specifies whether any modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window. Default: false

properties.name: at_rest_encryption_enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether to enable encryption at rest.

properties.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade
properties.description: Specifies whether minor engine upgrades are applied automatically to the cache cluster during the maintenance window. Default: true

properties.name: automatic_failover_enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether a read-only replica is automatically promoted to read/write primary if the existing primary fails. Default: true

properties.name: availability_zones
properties.description: List of Availability Zones in which cache nodes are created. terraform attribute: preferred_cache_cluster_azs

properties.name: cluster_mode
properties.description: Enables data partitioning across distinct nodes in a Redis (cluster mode enabled) replication group.

properties.name: engine
properties.description: The name of the cache engine to be used for the cache clusters in this replication group.

properties.name: engine_version
properties.description: The version number of the cache engine to be used for the cache clusters in this replication group.

properties.name: maintenance_window
properties.description: The weekly time range (in UTC) during which system maintenance can occur. Format: ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi

properties.name: multi_az_enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether to enable Multi-AZ Support for the replication group

properties.name: node_type
properties.description: Instance class for the cache cluster. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/red-ug/CacheNodes.SupportedTypes.html

properties.name: notification_topic_arn
properties.description: ARN of an SNS topic to send ElastiCache notifications to.

properties.name: number_cache_clusters
properties.description: The number of cache clusters (primary and replicas) in this replication group. Terraform attribute: num_cache_clusters

properties.name: parameter_group_name
properties.description: The name of the parameter group to associate with this replication group. If this argument is omitted, the default cache parameter group for the specified engine is used.

properties.name: port
properties.description: The port number on which each of the cache nodes accepts connections. Default: 6379

properties.name: replication_group_id
properties.description: The replication group identifier.

properties.name: replication_group_description
properties.description: The replication group description. Default: 'elasticache cluster'

properties.name: security_group_ids
properties.description: List of security group IDs to associate with this replication group.

properties.name: snapshot_retention_limit
properties.description: The number of days for which ElastiCache retains automatic cache cluster snapshots before deleting them. For zero (0), backups are turned off.

properties.name: snapshot_window
properties.description: The daily time range (in UTC) during which ElastiCache begins taking a daily snapshot of the node group. Minimum a 60 minute period. Format: hh24:mi-hh24:mi

properties.name: subnet_group_name
properties.description: The name of the cache subnet group to be used for the replication group.

properties.name: transit_encryption_enabled
properties.description: Specifies whether to enable encryption in transit.

properties.name: transit_encryption_mode
properties.description: Specifies whether to use encryption in transit for the replication group.

properties.name: service_updates_enabled
properties.description: Enable or disable automatic service updates for the ElastiCache cluster. Default: true

properties.name: service_updates_types
properties.description: A list of the service update types that will be applied automatically for the replication group. Default: ['security-update', 'engine-update']

properties.name: service_updates_severities
properties.description: A list of the service update severity levels that can be applied for the replication group. Default: ['critical', 'important']

properties.name: service_updates_cooldown_days
properties.description: The number of days that ElastiCache will wait before applying a service update to the replication group. Default: 14 days for production, 7 days for staging, and 5 days for others.

aws/group-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining AWS groups. Groups represent collections of users
or entities within an AWS account, along with associated policies
and metadata.

properties.name: account
properties.description:
Reference to the AWS account associated with the group. This links
the group to its parent account.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the group. This name is used to identify the group
within the AWS account.

properties.name: description
properties.description: A detailed description of the group, explaining its purpose and scope.

properties.name: policies
properties.description:
A list of policies associated with the group. Each policy defines
permissions and access controls for the group.

properties.name: connector_configuration
properties.description: Kafka Connect connector configuration properties (key-value pairs, plugin-specific)

properties.name: kafka_connect_version
properties.description: Kafka Connect version (default 3.7.1, set by ERv2 module)

properties.name: capacity
properties.description: Connector capacity configuration (default provisioned 1 MCU 1 worker, set by ERv2 module)

properties.name: worker_configuration
properties.description: Optional multiline worker configuration properties

properties.name: log_delivery
properties.description: Log delivery configuration (optional)

properties.name: payerAccount
properties.description: AWS parent account

properties.name: ou
properties.description: AWS organizational unit. Use path to the OU, e.g. /Root/OrgUnit1/Foo/Bar

properties.name: tags
properties.description: Tags for this account

properties.name: mandatory
properties.description: is policy mandatory in the account

properties.name: auth
properties.description: Authentication configuration for the proxy. We currently only support auth via secret, hence the required fields.

properties.name: connection_borrow_timeout
properties.description: The number of seconds for a proxy to wait for a connection to become available in the connection pool. Terraform attribute: connection_borrow_timeout

properties.name: db_instance_identifier
properties.description: DB instance identifier. The identifier of the RDS DB instance target

properties.name: debug_logging
properties.description: Whether the proxy includes detailed information about SQL statements in its logs. Default: false

properties.name: engine_family
properties.description: The kinds of databases that the proxy can connect to. This value determines which database network protocol the proxy recognizes when it interprets network traffic to and from the database

properties.name: iam_role_force_detach_policies
properties.description: Specifies to force detaching any policies the IAM role has before destroying it. Default: true

properties.name: iam_role_max_session_duration
properties.description: The maximum session duration (in seconds) for the IAM role. Default: 43200

properties.name: idle_client_timeout
properties.description: The number of seconds that a connection to the proxy can be inactive before the proxy disconnects it. Default: 1800

properties.name: init_query
properties.description: One or more SQL statements for the proxy to run when opening each new database connection

properties.name: log_group_retention_in_days
properties.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the CloudWatch log group. Default: 30

properties.name: max_connections_percent
properties.description: The maximum size of the connection pool for each target in a target group. Default: 90

properties.name: max_idle_connections_percent
properties.description: Controls how actively the proxy closes idle database connections in the connection pool. Default: 50

properties.name: require_tls
properties.description: A Boolean parameter that specifies whether Transport Layer Security (TLS) encryption is required for connections to the proxy. Default: true

properties.name: session_pinning_filters
properties.description: Each item in the list represents a class of SQL operations that normally cause all later statements in a session using a proxy to be pinned to the same underlying database connection

properties.name: vpc_security_group_ids
properties.description: One or more VPC security group IDs to associate with the new proxy

properties.name: vpc_subnet_ids
properties.description: One or more VPC subnet IDs to associate with the new proxy

aws/sharing-option-1.yml.description: define sharing options between aws accounts

properties.name: provider
properties.description: type of sharing to implement

properties.name: regex
properties.description: regex expression to filter items by

properties.name: repository
properties.description: GitLab repository URL

properties.name: ref
properties.description: commit SHA, not a branch like main or master

properties.name: projectPath
properties.description: path in the repo where the terraform files are located

properties.name: delete
properties.description: set to true to delete the repo

properties.name: requireFips
properties.description: whether this repo should be validated to ensure it is using FIPS endpoints for AWS

properties.name: tfVersion
properties.description: Which version of terraform binary to use

properties.name: forceRerunTimestamp
properties.description: force a rerun of tf-repo using ISO8601 format which can be grabbed with https://www.utctime.net/

properties.name: variables
properties.description: Vault paths defining where Terraform inputs are read from and where Terraform outputs are written to

properties.name: grafana_datasource
properties.description: is rds instance used as a grafana datasource

properties.name: enable_http2
properties.description: indicates whether HTTP/2 is enabled

properties.name: ingress_cidr_blocks
properties.description: ingress cidr blocks to allow for an alb

properties.name: managed_by_erv2
properties.description: Manage the resource with erv2

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Flag to delete the resource

properties.name: max_session_duration
properties.description:
Maximum session duration (in seconds) that you want to set for the specified role.
If you do not specify a value for this setting, the default maximum of one hour is applied.
This setting can have a value from 3600 (1 hour) to 43200 (12 hours).

properties.name: secret_format
properties.description:
A jinja2 templated object to define the shape of the secret to be created in Secrets
Manager instead of copying the vault secret as is.

properties.name: msk_cluster
properties.description: Identifier of the MSK cluster resource in the same externalResources block

properties.name: service_execution_role
properties.description: Identifier of the aws-iam-role resource in the same externalResources block

aws/terraform-resource-lifecycle-1.yml.description: meta-arguments available for all resource blocks regardless of type

properties.name: create_before_destroy
properties.description: create replacement object before the prior object is destroyed

properties.name: prevent_destroy
properties.description: reject with an error any plan that would destroy the object

properties.name: ignore_changes
properties.description: attributes to ignore when planning updates to the object

properties.name: cidr_block
properties.description: Reference to a network reservation file

properties.name: vpc_tags
properties.description: Additional tags for the VPC.

cloudflare/account-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a Cloudflare account managed via app-interface.
This schema captures all metadata, configuration, credentials, and
ownership information required to manage Cloudflare accounts in a
declarative and automated way. It supports integration with Terraform,
secure credential management, and compliance requirements such as
two-factor enforcement and deletion approvals.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the Cloudflare account. Used to identify the
account across the system and in automation.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the Cloudflare account, its
intended use, or any relevant context.

properties.name: accountOwners
properties.description:
A list of individuals or teams responsible for this Cloudflare
account. Each entry must include a name and email address for
contact and accountability.

properties.name: apiCredentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the API credentials for
this Cloudflare account. Used by automation and integrations to
securely access the Cloudflare API.

properties.name: enforceTwofactor
properties.description:
If true, enforces two-factor authentication for all users in this
Cloudflare account to meet security and compliance requirements.

cloudflare/account-defaults-1.yml.description: Cloudflare Account default file, used as input for er-cloudflare-account module.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Account name

properties.name: enforce_twofactor
properties.description: Indicates whether membership in this account requires that Two-Factor Authentication is enabled

cloudflare/dns-record-1.yml.description:
Schema for defining a DNS record in a Cloudflare-managed zone.
This schema supports all standard DNS record types, including
A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, NS, MX, CAA, DS, and DNSKEY. It enables
declarative management of DNS records, including advanced DNSSEC
and proxying options, for automation and compliance in
app-interface-managed Cloudflare zones.

properties.name: identifier
properties.description:
The unique identifier for this DNS record. Used to reference
and manage the record within app-interface and automation.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The DNS name for this record (e.g., 'www.example.com').
This is the hostname or subdomain to which the record applies.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The DNS record type. Supported types include A, AAAA, CAA,
CNAME, TXT, NS, MX, DS (for DNSSEC signing key), and DNSKEY
(for DNSSEC key records).

properties.name: ttl
properties.description:
The time-to-live (TTL) for this DNS record, in seconds.
Determines how long the record is cached by resolvers.

properties.name: content
properties.description:
The content for this DNS record. For most record types, this
is the target hostname, IP address, or record data.

properties.name: data
properties.description:
Additional data for advanced DNS record types, such as DS
and DNSKEY records for DNSSEC. Includes algorithm, key tag,
flags, protocol, public key, digest type, digest, tag, and value.

properties.name: proxied
properties.description:
If true, enables Cloudflare proxying for this DNS record.
Only applicable to certain record types (e.g., A, AAAA, CNAME).

properties.name: priority
properties.description: The priority for MX records. Lower values indicate higher priority.

cloudflare/dns-zone-defaults-1.yml.description: Cloudflare DNS Zone default file, used as input for er-cloudflare-zone module.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The domain name.

properties.name: plan
properties.description: The ID of the rate plan.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
A full zone implies that DNS is hosted with Cloudflare.
A partial zone is typically a partner-hosted zone or a CNAME setup.

cloudflare/rule-1.yml.description:
A rule defines a filter and an action to perform on the incoming requests that match the filter.
Doc: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ruleset-engine/about/rules/
Schema based on https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudflare/cloudflare/latest/docs/resources/ruleset#nestedatt--rules

properties.name: action
properties.description: The action to perform when the rule matches.

properties.name: expression
properties.description: The expression defining which traffic will match the rule.

properties.name: description
properties.description: An informative description of the rule.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether the rule should be executed.

properties.name: ref
properties.description: The reference of the rule (the rule's ID by default).

properties.name: action_parameters
properties.description: The parameters configuring the rule's action.

cloudflare/ruleset-1.yml.description:
A ruleset is an ordered set of rules that you can apply to traffic on the Cloudflare global network.
Rulesets belong to a phase and can only execute in the same phase.
Doc: https://developers.cloudflare.com/ruleset-engine/about/rulesets/
Schema based on https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudflare/cloudflare/latest/docs/resources/ruleset

properties.name: identifier
properties.description: The unique identifier for this ruleset.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The human-readable name of the ruleset.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: The kind of the ruleset.

properties.name: phase
properties.description: The phase of the ruleset.

properties.name: description
properties.description: An informative description of the ruleset.

properties.name: rules
properties.description: A list of rules to include in the ruleset.

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Flag a resource to be deleted

properties.name: managed_by_erv2
properties.description: Manage the resource with erv2

dependencies/acs-instance-1.yml.description:
Schema for referencing an Advanced Cluster Security (ACS) instance as a dependency.
This schema enables app-interface to reference ACS instances for integration and
configuration purposes. It does not provision or manage the lifecycle of the ACS
instance itself. Use this schema to declare connection details, credentials, and
authentication provider configuration for ACS integrations.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the ACS instance. Used to identify and reference
this instance in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the ACS instance, its environment,
or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The URL of the ACS API endpoint. Used by integrations and automation
to connect to the ACS instance.

properties.name: credentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing the credentials required to
authenticate to this ACS instance.

properties.name: authProvider
properties.description:
Configuration for the authentication provider used by this ACS instance.
Supports OIDC and other provider types for integration and access control.

dependencies/container-image-mirror-1.ym.description:
Schema for referencing a container image mirror as a dependency.
This schema allows app-interface to declare external container image
mirrors for use in automation and deployment workflows. It does not
provision or manage the lifecycle of the mirror itself. Use this schema
to specify the mirror URL, credentials, and tag filtering for integration.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The URL of the container image mirror. This is the registry or
repository endpoint from which images can be pulled.

properties.name: pullCredentials
properties.description:
Reference to the Vault secret containing credentials required to
pull images from this mirror. Used for authentication to private
or restricted mirrors.

properties.name: tags
properties.description:
A list of image tags to include from this mirror. Only images
matching these tags will be considered for use.

properties.name: tagsExclude
properties.description:
A list of image tags to exclude from this mirror. Images matching
these tags will be ignored, even if included by the 'tags' field.

dependencies/dependency-1.yml.description:
Schema for referencing an external or internal dependency in app-interface.
This schema enables teams to declare dependencies for integration, monitoring,
and documentation purposes. It does not provision or manage the lifecycle of
the dependency itself. Use this schema to describe the dependency's name,
operational model, statefulness, SLA, monitoring, and failure impact.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the dependency. Used to identify and reference
this dependency in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the dependency, its purpose,
or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: statefulness
properties.description:
The statefulness classification of the dependency. 'Durable' indicates
persistent state, 'Cache' indicates ephemeral state, and 'Stateless'
indicates no state is maintained.

properties.name: opsModel
properties.description:
The operational model for the dependency. 'Hosted' means managed by a
third party, 'External' means outside the organization, and 'Internal'
means managed within the organization.

properties.name: statusPage
properties.description:
The URL of the status page for this dependency, if available. Used for
monitoring uptime and incident reporting.

properties.name: SLA
properties.description:
The service level agreement (SLA) for this dependency. Defines the
expected reliability, uptime, and support commitments.

properties.name: dependencyFailureImpact
properties.description:
The impact on your service if this dependency fails. Used for risk
assessment and incident response planning.

properties.name: monitoring
properties.description:
Monitoring configuration for this dependency. Supports specifying a
direct monitoring URL or referencing an OpenShift namespace and
resource for monitoring integration.

properties.name: _target_namespace_zone
properties.description: a namespace and a route53 zone name provisioned within that namespace

properties.name: allowed_vault_secret_paths
properties.description: List of paths used to limit the secrets that can be accessed for creating DNS records, the primary use case being domain control validation (DCV)

properties.name: environmentID
properties.description: Dynatrace tenant ID.

properties.name: environmentUrl
properties.description: The URL to a specific Dynatrace entity called environment, which is also known as tenant.

properties.name: bootstrapToken
properties.description: Dynatrace API token dedicated for generating new API tokens for Hypershift.

properties.name: environment
properties.description: Which of integration, stage or production are the data coming from.

properties.name: dynatraceRegion
properties.description: https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/service/app-interface/-/blame/9f303046645ebbb48e87ccf5d89778521a2d8345/data/services/osd-operators/cicd/saas/saas-dynatrace-operator.yaml#L356-433

properties.name: awsRegion
properties.description: A list of HCP AWS regions.

dependencies/endpoint-monitoring-provide.description:
Schema for referencing an endpoint monitoring provider as a dependency in app-interface.
This schema enables teams to declare monitoring providers for endpoints, supporting
integration with blackbox-exporter and signalfx. Use this schema to specify provider type,
check intervals, timeouts, metric labels, and provider-specific configuration for
endpoint monitoring automation and observability.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The unique name of the endpoint monitoring provider. Used to identify and
reference this provider in app-interface and related integrations.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A human-readable description of the endpoint monitoring provider, its
purpose, or any relevant operational context.

properties.name: provider
properties.description:
The provider defines which monitoring implementation will be used for endpoints.
Supported values are 'blackbox-exporter' and 'signalfx'.

properties.name: timeout
properties.description:
Timeout for the monitoring check (e.g., '10s', '5m'). Specifies how long
to wait for a check to complete before considering it failed.

properties.name: checkInterval
properties.description:
Interval between endpoint checks (e.g., '10s', '1m'). Determines how
frequently the monitoring provider will check the endpoint.

properties.name: metricLabels
properties.description:
Labels to be added to the resulting Prometheus metrics. Used for
categorization and filtering of metrics in observability systems.

properties.name: blackboxExporter
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the blackbox-exporter provider.
Used when 'provider' is set to 'blackbox-exporter'.

properties.name: signalFx
properties.description:
Configuration options specific to the signalfx provider.
Used when 'provider' is set to 'signalfx'.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the feature toggle

properties.name: description
properties.description: A description of the feature toggle

properties.name: delete
properties.description: Whether to delete the feature toggle

properties.name: unleash
properties.description: Configuration for an Unleash feature toggle

properties.name: eventThrottleRate
properties.description: Percentage of new events to deny. 0-100

dependencies/jira-severity-priority-mapp.description: mappings between alert severities and jira ticket priorities

a list of providers that can influence the status of this

properties.name: integrations

properties.name: name

properties.description: Docker image to use for the outputs secret container

properties.description: Docker image to use for the outputs secret container

properties.name: outputs_secret_version
properties.description: Version of the outputs secret container to use

properties.name: default_tags
properties.description: Default tags to apply to all managed external resources.
